title: ADT Adapter Envelope Schema (Layer 2)
description: Structured metadata carried alongside the task intake. Contains tool identity, session metadata, and credential tags (descriptive only — never credential values). Stripped before reaching ADT Core Layer 3.

tool_session_id.description: Opaque tool-specific session identifier. Used for audit trail; carries no authority.

tool_capability_tags.description: Descriptive tags of tool capabilities. NOT authority grants — presence does not imply permission.

session_principal.description: The identity under which the tool session is running (tag only, e.g., 'xiaohe', 'alice@example.com'). NOT the Human Holder.

claimed_actor.description: The actor the session claims to represent (tag only). May equal session_principal. NOT the Human Holder.

credential_tags.description: Tag descriptions of available credentials (e.g., 'github_pat', 'openai_api_key'). NEVER contains actual credential values. Actual credentials exist ONLY in Layer 1.

independence_evidence.description: Two-phase Checker independence evidence. May be null for non-Checker roles. See ADT_RUNTIME_ADAPTER_CONTRACT.md §7.

separate_execution_context.description: Phase 1: Checker runs in a separate execution context.

maker_context_inherited.description: Phase 1: MUST be false. Checker does NOT inherit Maker's session state.

participated_in_implementation.description: Phase 2: MUST be false. Checker did not design, write, or contribute.

independent_fact_read.description: Phase 2: MUST be true. Checker read facts from independent sources.

independent_conclusion.description: Phase 2: MUST be true. Checker reached its own conclusion. NOT required pre-execution.
